summary: Checked the full Human-in-the-Loop-Pause diff against guides/backend-guide.md and guides/ui-style-guide.md. BACKEND — all clean. New services Phases::Pause, Phases::RerunMenuStep, Phases::RestartDefine follow the mandated shape: verb-first names, single public `self.call` -> Result with keyword args, domain failures returned as Result.failure symbols (:not_pausable/:busy/:not_paused/:invalid_artifact) rather than raised for flow control (backend-guide 'Services', 'Errors & results'); RestartDefine owns its multi-write atomicity in ApplicationRecord.transaction and every service broadcasts (BroadcastColumn/BroadcastCard) after the write, from the service layer, per 'side-effect ordering' and 'Real-time (Turbo) conventions'. Controllers stay thin: PhasesController#pause/#rerun_step/#restart and the widened ApprovalsController each authenticate via membership_scoped_phase, call one service, and branch only on result.success? to pick a redirect; the error->message mappers (menu_alert/pause_notice/approval_alert) are private presentation helpers, not business branching ('Controllers — light'). Models gain only persistence-intrinsic changes: the `paused` string enum on Phase, `restart_complete` string enum on ManagerDecision, and the `any_step_active?` predicate — no business-logic callbacks ('Models'). Phases::Convergence lives in app/queries/ as a read-only, side-effect-free predicate (the same rule ManagerTick uses, now shared), consistent with 'Query objects' for reporting reads. Migration 20260718230012 is schema-only and reversible (`change`), with DB-level `null: false` defaults mirroring the app's assumptions ('DB constraints mirror critical validations'). Minitest coverage accompanies every new/changed unit — pause/rerun_menu_step/restart_define/convergence/manager_tick services, phase model, define_helper, and the two controllers ('Testing'). UI — all clean. `paused` maps to the amber 'attention' tone via the single shared STATUS_TONES map in status_helper.rb (semantic status color, ui-style-guide 'Color' table: paused phases = amber); in-progress restart/re-run banners use blue-50/blue-600 (the 'running / in progress' semantic), and the failure line uses red-700 with a status_badge('failed', label: 'Re-run failed') so status is never color-alone ('Accessibility baseline'). Buttons match the component spec verbatim — primary Done `bg-indigo-600 ... hover:bg-indigo-700 rounded-md px-3 py-2 text-sm font-semibold`, secondary menu actions `bg-white ring-1 ring-inset ring-gray-300 hover:bg-gray-50` — all with `data-turbo-submits-with` for disable-on-submit, the destructive 'Repeat from the Beginning' gated by `turbo_confirm`, and progress messages in `aria-live="polite"` regions ('Core components', 'Real-time behavior', 'Accessibility'). The paused card reuses the existing `rounded-lg border bg-white p-6 shadow-sm` card and the shared status_badge (one source of truth). Non-blocking observation (not a finding): a few new elements use step-3 spacing (`gap-3`, `mt-3`) which is outside the guide's stated scale of {2,4,6,8,12}; this is a pre-existing, codebase-wide convention already present in this same partial before the change (approval/answers forms) and across ~10 other view files, so it is consistent existing drift rather than a deviation this change introduces — carried forward exactly as the Plan-phase guide-alignment review treated the analogous sub-heading case.
